Misc Notes

Parker Howell

Last Updated: 2023-11-28

1 General

1.0.1 Thm “concave then [local iff global]”

If \(f: X \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\) is concave (convex), then \(x^*\) is a local max (min) if and only if \(x\) is a global max (min).

2 Analysis

2.0.1 Def Injective (one-to-one)

\[x_1 \neq x_2 \Rightarrow f(x_1) \neq f(x_2)\]

2.0.2 Def Surjective (onto)

\[??\]

3 Misc

Price Elasticity, \(\varepsilon = F'(p)p / F(p) = \frac{\Delta x / \Delta p}{x / p}\) (aka marginal demand (\(\Delta x / \Delta p\)) times average demand \(x/p\))

Inelastic: \(-1 < \varepsilon < 0\)

Elastic: \(-\infty < \varepsilon < -1\)

If elastic, a price increase increases total expenditure. If inelastic, a price increase decreases total expenditure.
